reasoning: The overarching policy of the Chinese government regarding decentralized cryptocurrencies has been consistently restrictive to prevent capital flight and preserve monetary control. While crypto influencers periodically spread rumors about an impending 'unban,' these have been repeatedly debunked and typically coincide with market manipulation attempts. Sourced information up to August 2025 confirms that China shows absolutely no signs of reversing its ban on trading Bitcoin with yuan; in fact, recent updates suggest an even tighter crackdown, such as updating Anti-Money Laundering laws to target illegal crypto transactions. The requirement for an explicit PRC announcement that citizens can 'legally buy Bitcoin with yuan from inside China' makes this extremely unlikely to occur by the end of 2025. Given the lack of credible signals and the persistence of the restrictive status quo, the probability is at the minimum threshold.
